workflows.name: BugDB
workflows.description: Read, comment, or create BugDB bugs.
workflows.context: BugDB-only workflow. Use BugDB MCP for existing bug reads, summaries, updates, and comments. Use the local bugdb_create_bug.py helper only for new bug creation.

operations.name: Summarize Bug
operations.description: Retrieve the bug through BugDB MCP and provide a concise summary.

operations.name: Add Private Comment
operations.description: Retrieve the bug through BugDB MCP, then add the requested hidden/private comment through MCP.

operations.name: Create Bug
operations.description: Create a new BugDB bug using the local helper. Defaults come from bugdb_create_payload.json.

operations.name: Custom BugDB Task
operations.description: Run a custom BugDB-only request. Existing bugs must use MCP where possible.

workflows.name: ADE
workflows.description: ADE view, transaction, OraReview, merge request, and cleanup workflows.
workflows.context: ADE-only workflow. Use the ade-lens, orareview, and mergereq skills where relevant. If a selected skill reports a newer version, install it before continuing without asking for separate approval. Use CodeSearch, OraReview, and Mergereq MCP tools for read-only lookup and report active view, active transaction, command outcomes, and next step.
requirements: If ade-lens reports a newer version is available, install the update automatically before ADE work and continue without asking for approval.
Use the orareview skill for OraReview upload, status, close, and reviewer-comment workflows.
Use the mergereq skill for mergereq submission, quality, property, and post-approval merge workflows.
Use CodeSearch MCP for ADE/OpenGrok code searches.
Use OraReview MCP tools for read-only review metadata, reviewer status, comments, and merge-check details.
Use Mergereq MCP tools for read-only report-card status and current-transaction quality previews.
Do not run ade_codex from the GitHub runner; this is already a non-interactive codex exec session.
Run ADE commands from the normal login home/ADE environment, not a temporary HOME.
For ADE view and transaction setup, prefer non-interactive ADE Lens scripts such as CreateViewAndTxn.sh and RunInView.sh.
If Create View has no transaction name, generate a simple transaction name from the view name and current UTC timestamp.
Create real ADE views in normal ADE storage so they are visible to ordinary ade lsviews.
If ADE falls back to /tmp/rnerolu_ade_* or reports sandbox write limits, stop and report failure instead of claiming the view was created.
Bind the work to exactly one ADE view.
Use ADE commands as the source of truth for view and transaction state.
Do not edit source files unless the additional inputs explicitly request source changes.

operations.name: Create View
operations.description: Create or enter an ADE view and report the active transaction.

operations.name: Enter Existing View
operations.description: Enter an existing ADE view and report its current state.

operations.name: Check View Status
operations.description: Inspect ADE view, transaction, and reservation status.

operations.name: Create Transaction
operations.description: Create or enter a transaction inside the selected ADE view.

operations.name: Refresh View
operations.description: Refresh the selected ADE view when appropriate.

operations.name: CodeSearch
operations.description: Search ADE/OpenGrok through the CodeSearch MCP.

operations.name: Check In And Save
operations.description: Check in and save the active ADE transaction.

operations.name: Create OraReview
operations.description: Create or update OraReview for the selected ADE view and bug.

operations.name: Submit Merge Request
operations.description: Submit or manage a merge request for the selected ADE transaction.

operations.name: Cleanup View
operations.description: Clean up an ADE view or transaction only when explicitly requested.

operations.name: Custom ADE Task
operations.description: Run a custom ADE-only task with ade-lens.

workflows.name: Project Work
workflows.description: Inspect, fix, or test a configured local ALM checkout.
workflows.context: Local project workflow. Work only in the selected local project path unless the task explicitly widens scope.

operations.name: Inspect
operations.description: Inspect the selected project and report findings.

operations.name: Fix
operations.description: Make a scoped fix in the selected project and run relevant checks.

operations.name: Test
operations.description: Run requested tests or checks in the selected project.

operations.name: Custom Project Task
operations.description: Run a custom task in the selected project.

workflows.name: Custom Commands
workflows.description: Run configured command intents or request approval for a natural-language command plan.
workflows.context: Custom command workflow. Use the selected run context and the validated command catalog. Catalog commands are approved to run in order. Skill updates are pre-approved for GitHub runner execution; if a selected skill reports a newer version, install it before continuing without asking for separate approval. For natural-language custom tasks, infer the relevant skill or MCP from the request text, project context, and available ADE or transaction details. Use read-only MCP tools directly for information gathering, status, report-card, review-comment, and code-search questions. For natural-language state-changing requests without catalog commands, propose a numbered command sequence first and ask for approval with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. After approval arrives in a follow-up comment and the issue is tagged followup-codex, continue in the same session and run the approved sequence.

operations.name: Run Commands
operations.description: Run selected command-catalog intents, or propose and request approval for a natural-language command plan.

projects.name: Codex Automation
projects.context: Use the codex_automation repository for runner, UI, and helper-script work.

projects.name: ADE Home
projects.context: Use the normal login home/ADE environment for ADE command orchestration.

projects.name: Employment Process UI
projects.description: Employment process UI local ALM checkout.
projects.context: Use the employment-process-ui local checkout. Treat this as the selected ALM project context for code inspection, fixes, tests, and VBCS/Fusion HCM UI work.

projects.name: Employment Other UI
projects.description: Employment other UI local ALM checkout.
projects.context: Use the employment-other-ui local checkout. Treat this as the selected ALM project context for code inspection, fixes, tests, and VBCS/Fusion HCM UI work.

projects.name: Employment All
projects.description: Parent folder for multiple employment UI projects.
projects.context: Use the employment-all aggregate context when the task needs to compare or search across employment-process-ui and employment-other-ui.
